dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: That was Drifter space. Those were my people's routes.

dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: You handled it well, {captain}. Most wouldn't bother.

captain_options.label: [Acknowledge] 'I know what that territory means to you.'

outcome.narrative: Oss looks at you for a long moment. Something shifts behind her eyes — not warmth, exactly, but the absence of a wall that was there before.

captain_options.label: [Dismiss] 'We had a job to do. That's all.'

outcome.narrative: Oss's jaw tightens. She nods once and turns back to her console without another word.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: I used to draft the protocols for encounters like that. Every word calibrated.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: Funny how different it looks from this side of the negotiating table.

captain_options.label: [Probe] 'Sounds like you have a history with the Assembly.'

outcome.narrative: Kess gives you a look that says she's deciding how much to trust you. 'History is one word for it,' she says. The conversation is over, but the door isn't closed.

captain_options.label: [Professional] 'We got through it. That's what matters.'

outcome.narrative: Kess nods crisply. The bureaucrat mask slides back into place.

dialogue.speaker: vel_remnant_scientist
dialogue.text: Interesting. The anomaly responded to your presence differently than I expected.

dialogue.speaker: vel_remnant_scientist
dialogue.text: Most organic minds cause static. Yours caused... recognition.

captain_options.label: [Curious] 'What do you mean, recognition?'

outcome.narrative: Vel tilts their head, studying you like a specimen. 'The Nexus remembers things, {captain}. I think it's starting to remember you.' The words should be unsettling. Coming from Vel, they sound almost like a compliment.

captain_options.label: [Wary] 'I'd rather it didn't.'

outcome.narrative: Vel's expression doesn't change. 'That is not how the Nexus works,' they say quietly. 'But I understand the instinct.'

dialogue.speaker: tam_technician
dialogue.text: Did you feel that? When the systems came online?

dialogue.speaker: tam_technician
dialogue.text: My implants were... resonating. Like they recognized the architecture.

dialogue.speaker: sera_soldier
dialogue.text: Vreth patrol markings. Those were Brennar garrison insignia.

dialogue.speaker: dorin_engineer
dialogue.text: Sera—

dialogue.speaker: sera_soldier
dialogue.text: I'm fine. Just... noting it.

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: Could've been worse. Could always be worse.

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: I've treated injuries like these before. On people who didn't make it.

dialogue.speaker: any_idealistic
dialogue.text: That didn't have to go the way it did. We could have found another path.

dialogue.speaker: any_idealistic
dialogue.text: I know you did what you thought was right, {captain}. I just hope it was worth the cost.

dialogue.speaker: any_pragmatic
dialogue.text: That cost us. Real resources, real damage.

dialogue.speaker: any_pragmatic
dialogue.text: Next time, let's count the odds before we jump.

dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: You watch exits. I watch escape routes. We should stop pretending we don't notice each other doing it.

dialogue.speaker: sera_soldier
dialogue.text: I watch exits because I was trained to. You watch them because you're afraid.

dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: Afraid? I'm a Drifter pilot. Fear is what keeps the hull between me and vacuum.

dialogue.speaker: sera_soldier
dialogue.text: Exactly my point. You fly like someone expecting the worst. That makes you either paranoid or smart.

dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: Can't it be both?

captain_options.label: [Mediate] 'You're both right. That's why this works.'

outcome.narrative: Oss and Sera exchange a glance. It's not friendship — not yet — but it's the beginning of respect.

captain_options.label: [Challenge] 'This isn't productive. Focus on the mission.'

outcome.narrative: They both fall silent. The tension doesn't resolve — it just goes underground.

dialogue.speaker: dorin_engineer
dialogue.text: You've been checking the bulkheads again. Third time this shift.

dialogue.speaker: sera_soldier
dialogue.text: The weld joints on frame seven are degrading. Standard maintenance.

dialogue.speaker: dorin_engineer
dialogue.text: Sera. That's not what I mean.

dialogue.speaker: sera_soldier
dialogue.text: I know what you mean, Dorin. The Vreth encounter rattled us both. I'm handling it.

dialogue.speaker: dorin_engineer
dialogue.text: You're handling it by not sleeping and checking bulkheads. That's not handling it.

captain_options.label: [Stay out] 'I'll let you two work this out.'

outcome.narrative: You step away. Through the closed hatch, you hear their voices — low, careful, honest. When they emerge later, something has settled between them.

captain_options.label: [Direct] 'Both of you need to stay sharp. We can't afford this.'

outcome.narrative: Dorin nods stiffly. Sera salutes — crisp, military, cold. They'll do their jobs. But the wall between them just got higher.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: You were Concord medical corps before the Compact, weren't you?

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: ...Yes. A long time ago.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: I read your file once. Back when I had access to those files.

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: Then you know why I left.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: I know why the file says you left. I'm asking why you actually left.

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: Because I couldn't look at my hands and see a doctor anymore.

dialogue.speaker: tam_technician
dialogue.text: Vel? My implants are doing that thing again. The harmonic.

dialogue.speaker: vel_remnant_scientist
dialogue.text: I know. I can feel it from across the ship.

dialogue.speaker: tam_technician
dialogue.text: You can feel my implants?

dialogue.speaker: vel_remnant_scientist
dialogue.text: I can feel what your implants are connected to. There is a difference.

dialogue.speaker: tam_technician
dialogue.text: That's... both amazing and deeply unsettling.

dialogue.speaker: vel_remnant_scientist
dialogue.text: Most truths about the Nexus are.

dialogue.speaker: dr_yema_osha
dialogue.text: Your field sutures are excellent. Military training?

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: Something like that. Your pathogen containment protocols are... thorough.

dialogue.speaker: dr_yema_osha
dialogue.text: I learned the hard way. My crew—

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: You don't have to explain. I know what it's like to carry that.

dialogue.speaker: dr_yema_osha
dialogue.text: Do you? Or do you just know what it's like to carry something different and call it the same?

captain_options.label: [Encourage] 'You both have experience. Use it together.'

outcome.narrative: Osha extends her hand. Rook takes it — carefully, like he's not sure he deserves to. Two medics who know too much about what happens when healing fails.

captain_options.label: [Redirect] 'We need medics, not therapists. Keep it professional.'

outcome.narrative: Both fall silent. They'll work together — they're professionals. But the moment of connection is gone.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: Your course deviation added six hours to our transit. Protocol says—

dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: Protocol says a lot of things. The asteroid field I dodged says I'm alive.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: You could have flagged it. I could have calculated—

dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: Calculated what? The optimal speed at which to fly into a rock? I don't do things by committee.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: That's exactly the problem.

captain_options.label: [Side with Oss] 'In the pilot's chair, instinct saves lives.'

outcome.narrative: Oss grins. Kess's lips thin, but she nods. 'Fine. But next time, a heads-up.' It's not agreement — it's a ceasefire.

captain_options.label: [Side with Kess] 'Communication keeps the crew safe. Both of you.'

outcome.narrative: Kess straightens. Oss rolls her eyes but doesn't argue. The diplomat won this round.

dialogue.speaker: any_reckless
dialogue.text: If we'd hit the boost sooner, we would've cleared that debris field clean.

dialogue.speaker: any_pragmatic
dialogue.text: If we'd hit the boost sooner, we would've lost the port stabilizer.

dialogue.speaker: any_reckless
dialogue.text: Maybe. But we'd have a better story.

dialogue.speaker: any_pragmatic
dialogue.text: I prefer stories where the ship is intact at the end.

dialogue.speaker: any_vocal
dialogue.text: You never talk about what happened before. Don't you think the crew deserves to know?

dialogue.speaker: any_haunted
dialogue.text: No.

dialogue.speaker: any_vocal
dialogue.text: That's it? Just 'no'?

dialogue.speaker: any_haunted
dialogue.text: Some things don't get better when you talk about them. Some things just get louder.

dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: {captain}. Got a minute?

dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: You asked about the Drifter routes. The truth is, I know those routes because I grew up on the freeports they connect.

dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: One of those freeports doesn't exist anymore. The Concord Assembly destroyed it. I was the only one who made it out.

dialogue.speaker: oss_navigator_pilot
dialogue.text: I'm the only proof it ever happened. That's why certain people want me quiet.

captain_options.label: [Support] 'You're safe on this ship. That's a promise.'

outcome.narrative: Oss searches your face for the lie. She doesn't find one. 'I'm going to hold you to that, {captain}.' For the first time, her voice doesn't sound like she's already planning her escape.

captain_options.label: [Honest] 'I can't promise safety. But I won't hand you over.'

outcome.narrative: Oss nods slowly. 'That's actually more reassuring than a promise. Promises break. Intentions don't — not from people like you.'

captain_options.label: [Practical] 'That's useful intel. The Concord won't expect us to know.'

outcome.narrative: Something shutters behind Oss's eyes. 'Right. Intel.' She stands and leaves. You've gained information. You may have lost something else.

dialogue.speaker: sera_soldier
dialogue.text: I need to tell you something about Brennar VI, {captain}.

dialogue.speaker: sera_soldier
dialogue.text: Those garrison insignia we saw — I recognized them because they were mine. I was the commanding officer.

dialogue.speaker: sera_soldier
dialogue.text: I was ordered to annex the settlement. I chose not to. The soldiers I commanded... I stopped them myself.

dialogue.speaker: sera_soldier
dialogue.text: Dorin doesn't know. He thinks the garrison just withdrew.

captain_options.label: [Accept] 'You made the right call. That's what matters now.'

outcome.narrative: Sera's posture breaks for just a moment — shoulders dropping, breath releasing. She straightens again, but something is different. Lighter. 'Thank you, {captain}. I needed to hear that from someone who isn't me.'

captain_options.label: [Question] 'Dorin deserves to know eventually.'

outcome.narrative: 'I know,' Sera says. 'I've known since Brennar. I just... I don't know how to be the person who saved him and the person who killed her own squad. Not to the same person.' She looks away. 'But you're right.'

captain_options.label: [Cold] 'Your past is your problem. Just do your job.'

outcome.narrative: Sera snaps back to attention. 'Yes, Captain.' The walls go up — thick, military-grade. You won't be hearing from her again.

dialogue.speaker: dorin_engineer
dialogue.text: You've seen how I work, {captain}. I fix things. That's what I do.

dialogue.speaker: dorin_engineer
dialogue.text: But I wasn't always a repair tech. Before the settlement, I was a Vreth military engineer.

dialogue.speaker: dorin_engineer
dialogue.text: I built drive systems. Good ones. The kind that let capital ships jump into civilian sectors before anyone can send a warning.

dialogue.speaker: dorin_engineer
dialogue.text: One of those drives was used to jump into Brennar VI. My home.

captain_options.label: [Compassion] 'You didn't pull the trigger. The Vreth did.'

outcome.narrative: Dorin's hands stop moving — the first time you've seen them still. 'I built the gun,' he says quietly. Then: 'But thank you for saying that.' He goes back to work. The silence between you feels different now. Warmer.

captain_options.label: [Direct] 'That knowledge is valuable here. Make it count.'

outcome.narrative: Dorin nods. Pragmatic to pragmatic. 'I plan to.' It's not absolution, but it's purpose. Sometimes that's enough.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: {captain}, you asked about my history with the Assembly. I think it's time you knew.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: I didn't just work for the Concord. I designed the Reclamation Division's operational protocols.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: Every hidden facility, every suppressed record, every officer who signed off on making evidence disappear — I wrote the playbook.

dialogue.speaker: kess_diplomat
dialogue.text: I left because they started using those protocols on people instead of documents.

captain_options.label: [Trust] 'That took courage. Both the leaving and the telling.'

outcome.narrative: Kess blinks — you've surprised her. Bureaucrats don't expect to be understood. 'I've been running from what I built for three years. Maybe it's time to start dismantling it instead.' Her eyes are sharp with purpose.

captain_options.label: [Strategic] 'Those protocols could be useful to us.'

outcome.narrative: Kess stiffens. 'I didn't tell you this so you could weaponize it.' A pause. 'But... yes. Knowing how they operate gives us an edge.' The diplomat in her recognizes the logic even as the person in her recoils.

dialogue.speaker: vel_remnant_scientist
dialogue.text: {captain}. I want to show you something.

dialogue.speaker: vel_remnant_scientist
dialogue.text: When I speak about the Nexus builders, I use present tense. You've noticed.

dialogue.speaker: vel_remnant_scientist
dialogue.text: Most assume it's an affectation. An academic quirk.

dialogue.speaker: vel_remnant_scientist
dialogue.text: It is not a quirk.

captain_options.label: [Gentle] 'Take your time. I'm listening.'

outcome.narrative: Vel's expression shifts — a crack in that unsettling patience. 'You are the first person in a very long time who has said that and meant it.' They don't explain further. Not yet. But the silence between you feels like a promise.

captain_options.label: [Press] 'Are you saying you're one of them?'

outcome.narrative: Vel tilts their head. 'I am saying that the distinction between studying the builders and being one is less clear than you might assume.' A non-answer that answers everything. The Nexus hums faintly, as if in agreement.

dialogue.speaker: tam_technician
dialogue.text: Hey, {captain}? Can I ask you something weird?

dialogue.speaker: tam_technician
dialogue.text: My implants. I don't remember getting them. I woke up on that derelict and they were just... part of me.

dialogue.speaker: tam_technician
dialogue.text: They talk to machines. Not metaphorically — I can feel data like texture. Code like sound.

dialogue.speaker: tam_technician
dialogue.text: Do you think something was done to me? Or do you think I was always like this?

captain_options.label: [Reassure] 'Whatever you were before, you're crew now. That's what matters.'

outcome.narrative: Tam smiles — quick, bright, genuine. 'Yeah. Okay. That's a good answer.' She hops off the console and heads back to work, lighter. The question isn't answered, but it doesn't weigh as much anymore.

captain_options.label: [Curious] 'Have you tried interfacing with the ship's Nexus systems?'

outcome.narrative: Tam's eyes widen. 'I... no. I was afraid to.' A pause. 'But you're right. If I'm going to understand what I am, that might be where I start.' The curiosity in her voice wins over the fear. Barely.

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: {captain}. I owe you honesty.

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: Before the Compact, I was Concord medical corps. Surgical division. The kind that doesn't appear in public records.

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: I performed experimental procedures on Drifter prisoners. They told me it was research. I told myself it was necessary.

dialogue.speaker: rook_medic
dialogue.text: Those prisoners are still alive. Some of them are looking for me.

captain_options.label: [Compassion] 'People change. What you're doing now — that matters.'

outcome.narrative: Rook stares at the floor for a long time. When he looks up, his eyes are wet. 'Fifteen years of penance and you're the first person who's said that.' He takes a breath. 'I won't waste it, {captain}.'

captain_options.label: [Honest] 'That's a heavy burden. I can't absolve you, but I won't judge you.'

outcome.narrative: 'I'm not asking for absolution,' Rook says. 'I'm asking if you still want me on your ship.' The question hangs. You nod. He nods back. It's enough.

captain_options.label: [Hard] 'If your past endangers this crew, I need to know immediately.'

outcome.narrative: Rook flinches. 'Understood, Captain.' The military formality is back — a shield he knows how to wear. He'll keep you informed. He won't keep you close.

dialogue.speaker: dr_yema_osha
dialogue.text: {captain}, I need to correct something. About the pathogen that killed my research crew.

dialogue.speaker: dr_yema_osha
dialogue.text: I told you it was an accident. A containment failure. That was true — technically.

dialogue.speaker: dr_yema_osha
dialogue.text: What I didn't tell you is that my team engineered the pathogen. From Remnant biosamples. I led the research.

dialogue.speaker: dr_yema_osha
dialogue.text: I was the only one immune because I built the immunity into myself first. My crew didn't know the risks because I didn't tell them the risks.

captain_options.label: [Forgive] 'You've carried this long enough. Let it go.'

outcome.narrative: Osha's composure finally breaks. The idealist, the scientist, the woman who treats every patient like a penance — she cries. Quietly. Completely. When it passes, something has healed. Not everything. But enough. 'Thank you, {captain}. I'll spend the rest of my career making sure no one else makes my mistakes.'

captain_options.label: [Firm] 'That's a serious breach of trust. We'll discuss this further.'

outcome.narrative: Osha nods. She expected this. Maybe she needed it. 'I understand. I'll submit a full report on the pathogen and my immunity. Use it however you need.' Her voice is steady. The confession was the hard part.

dialogue.speaker: any_haunted
dialogue.text: {captain}. Can I be honest?

dialogue.speaker: any_haunted
dialogue.text: I'm not sure how much longer I can keep this up. The jumps, the fights, the constant uncertainty.

dialogue.speaker: any_haunted
dialogue.text: I signed on because I believed in what we're doing. I still do. But believing and enduring are different things.

captain_options.label: [Rally] 'We're in this together. I won't let this crew fall apart.'

outcome.narrative: A nod. Not conviction — not yet — but something close to it. 'Okay, {captain}. Okay.' They head back to their station. The weight is still there, but it's shared now.

captain_options.label: [Honest] 'I can't promise it gets easier. But I need you here.'

outcome.narrative: 'That's the most honest thing anyone's said to me in a while.' They almost smile. Almost. 'I'll stay. Just... don't forget we're people back here, not just stat sheets.'
